name: Cyclopts (Modern Python CLI Framework)

performance.notes: Comparable to click/typer; startup overhead for CLI applications

agent_readiness.notes: Modern Python CLI framework using type hints and attrs/pydantic-style validation. from cyclopts import App. app = App(). @app.command. def hello(name: str, count: int = 1): for _ in range(count): print(f'Hello {name}!'). app(). Subcommands: @app.command. @app.default for default command. Automatic help generation from type hints and docstrings. Enum choices. Literal choices. Union types for multiple accepted formats. Annotated with validators. async def support. Pydantic model parameters. Rich help formatting. Parameter aliases. Meta parameters (app --version, --help). app.meta.default for meta-level commands.

gotchas: Newer library — smaller ecosystem and community than click/typer
API may evolve — check changelog for breaking changes between minor versions
Pydantic integration requires pydantic to be installed
Limited third-party plugins vs click's extensive ecosystem
best_when: Modern CLI apps with full type annotation support and async
When Typer's click dependency or limitations are a concern
CLIs using Pydantic models as command parameters
Small to medium CLI tools with clean, type-safe code
avoid_when: When typer or click is already established in the codebase
Very complex CLIs needing advanced click features
summary: Cyclopts is a modern Python CLI framework using type hints. @app.command def hello(name: str, count: int = 1). Auto-help from type hints and docstrings. Enum/Literal choices. Async support. Pydantic models as parameters. Rich formatting. MIT by Brian Pugh. Type-annotated. Best for type-safe modern CLI apps. Newer than click/typer — smaller ecosystem. Good alternative to Typer with more features.
